description_long: This object is a classic corded telephone with a robust, angular base and a separate handset resting on top. The base is predominantly black with a textured, slightly glossy finish, and features a large, circular beige rotary dial on the front. A coiled black cord connects the handset to the left side of the base, adding a distinctive visual element. The handset is shaped to fit comfortably in the hand and sits securely in a molded cradle on the base. The phone's design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century models commonly found in homes and offices. The overall construction appears solid, with visible seams and a metallic or plastic build.
description: A vintage black corded telephone with a rotary dial, coiled cord, and a sturdy, textured base. The handset rests in a cradle atop the base, connected by a visible wire.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handset resting on the cradle and the coiled cord extending from the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base and the profile of the handset, with a hint of the rotary dial visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the circular rotary dial prominently and the coiled cord trailing to the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the handset and the coiled cord extending outward.
